overview.tagline: An exclusive estate community of rolling hills, horse farms, and timeless elegance
overview.description: The Borough of Saddle River is one of Bergen County's most prestigious and affluent communities. Known for its expansive estate properties, equestrian culture, and strict two-acre minimum lot zoning, Saddle River maintains a rural, pastoral character that is unique among northern New Jersey suburbs. Home to prominent business leaders, celebrities, and professionals, the borough offers unmatched privacy and natural beauty just 25 miles from Midtown Manhattan.
overview.schoolDistrict: Saddle River Public Schools / Ramsey High School (sending district)
highlights: Two-acre minimum lot zoning preserves estate-like character
Equestrian-friendly community with horse farms and bridle paths
One of the wealthiest communities in the United States
Scenic Saddle River running through the borough
Exceptional privacy within 25 miles of Manhattan

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Due to the high value and unique nature of Saddle River estates, individual assessments can vary significantly.
realEstate.recentTrends: Saddle River's luxury market has seen renewed interest as affluent buyers seek privacy and space. Properties range from $1.5 million to well over $10 million, with significant variation based on lot size, home condition, and amenities. The market moves slowly due to limited inventory and the exclusive nature of the community.
neighborhoodNotes: East Saddle River Road — Grand estates along the main thoroughfare
West Saddle River Road — Secluded properties with wooded lots
Chestnut Ridge Road area — Elevated properties with panoramic views
Woodcliff area — Homes near the Woodcliff Lake border
Northern section — Horse farms and large rural estates

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Private trash collection is arranged by individual homeowners in Saddle River. The borough does not provide municipal trash service.
details: Homeowners contract with private haulers for trash collection
Several licensed haulers serve the borough
Collection schedules vary by private provider
Bulk item removal arranged directly with haulers
Borough recycling drop-off available at the DPW yard

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Recycling services are available through both private haulers and the municipal recycling depot.
details: Municipal recycling depot available for residents
Private haulers also collect recyclables
Yard waste must be composted on-site or removed by private haulers
Electronics and hazardous waste handled through Bergen County programs
Leaf collection conducted by DPW in autumn months

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Most Saddle River properties use private wells and septic systems. Some areas are served by public water from Veolia Water.
details: Many properties rely on private wells for water supply
Septic systems are standard for most homes; no municipal sewer system
Properties with well water must conduct regular testing
Septic systems must be inspected and pumped regularly
Some newer developments may have public water connections

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department oversees construction permits and zoning compliance, with particular attention to preserving the borough's estate character.
details: Building permits required for all construction and major renovations
Strict two-acre minimum zoning enforced throughout the borough
Environmental review required for projects near wetlands or waterways
Historical and architectural review for significant changes
Tree removal permits required for mature trees

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Parking in Saddle River is primarily on private property. The borough has minimal public parking facilities given its residential estate character.
details: No metered or commercial parking areas in the borough
Street parking is prohibited on most borough roads
Residents park on their own properties
Visitor parking at Borough Hall for municipal business
Snow emergency parking restrictions apply

additionalServices.title: Saddle River Public Library
additionalServices.description: Small community library offering personalized service and programs.
details: Located within Borough Hall complex
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Children's story hours and reading programs
Community meeting space available

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Saddle River maintains strict noise regulations to preserve the quiet, rural character of the community.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 8:00 AM and after 6:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to specific hours
Generator noise and other mechanical equipment must meet decibel limits

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain the appearance and condition of their estate properties.
keyRules: Properties must be maintained in good condition and neat appearance
Property owners responsible for maintaining road frontage and drainage
Dead or hazardous trees must be removed by property owners
Fences and walls must be maintained and comply with zoning setbacks
No storage of commercial equipment or materials on residential lots

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Saddle River permits horses and farm animals in addition to standard household pets, reflecting its equestrian heritage.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed or under voice control on public property
Horses permitted on properties meeting minimum lot size requirements
Farm animals such as chickens may be permitted with proper setbacks
Pet waste must be removed from public areas and neighboring properties

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is restricted on borough roads to maintain safety and the rural character of the community.
keyRules: No parking on most borough roads at any time
Construction vehicles must park on the property being worked on
Snow emergency parking bans strictly enforced
No commercial vehicles parked on residential properties overnight
All vehicles must be registered and operable

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental of residential properties in Saddle River is subject to zoning and code requirements.
keyRules: Long-term rentals permitted in residential zones
Short-term rentals (under 30 days) are generally not permitted
Accessory apartments or in-law suites must comply with zoning
Rental properties must meet all building and safety codes
No subdivision of single-family properties for multiple rental units

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: Saddle River has strong tree preservation ordinances protecting the borough's wooded character.
keyRules: Permit required to remove trees above a specified caliper on private property
Replacement planting may be required when significant trees are removed
Environmental impact review for major tree removal projects
Protection of trees near waterways and wetlands is mandatory

other.title: Equestrian Regulations
other.summary: The borough supports equestrian use with specific regulations for horse keeping and riding.
keyRules: Horses permitted on properties with adequate acreage
Stable and paddock setbacks must meet zoning requirements
Manure management plans may be required
Riding on designated bridle paths and private property is permitted

schools.publicDistrict: Saddle River Public Schools (K-8) / Ramsey High School (9-12, sending district)

publicSchools.name: Wandell School
publicSchools.notes: The only public school in Saddle River; small class sizes and personalized education. Students attend Ramsey High School for grades 9-12.

privateSchools.name: Saddle River Day School
privateSchools.notes: Independent college-preparatory school known for innovative curriculum and small class sizes

privateSchools.name: The Christian Health Care Center (Academy)
privateSchools.notes: Faith-based educational programs within the Christian Health Care campus

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 15 minutes

whyMoveHere: Exceptional privacy with two-acre minimum lot zoning
Prestigious address in one of America's wealthiest communities
Equestrian-friendly environment with horse farms and bridle paths
Beautiful natural setting with mature trees and rolling terrain
Low-density living within easy reach of New York City
Excellent schools and safe, peaceful community

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus / Drive to rail station
commuteToNYC.notes: Saddle River residents typically drive to nearby NJ Transit stations (Ramsey Route 17, Allendale, or Waldwick) for train service on the Main/Bergen County Line to Hoboken, or use bus service. Many residents drive to Manhattan directly via Route 17 to I-80 or the Garden State Parkway. The commute ranges from 50-75 minutes depending on method and traffic.

costOfLiving.summary: Saddle River is among the most expensive communities in New Jersey, with luxury home prices and property taxes reflecting the estate-like character of the borough.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 150% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Unparalleled privacy and seclusion in a luxury setting
Estate-sized properties on wooded, scenic lots
Very low crime rate and tight-knit community
Strong property values in a highly exclusive market
cons: Extremely high home prices — entry point above $1.5 million
Very high property taxes on large estates
No walkable town center, shops, or restaurants
Private well and septic systems require maintenance

restaurants.name: Saddle River Inn
restaurants.description: Acclaimed fine dining restaurant set in a beautifully restored barn, offering contemporary French-American cuisine and an extensive wine list.

restaurants.name: Saddle River Cafe
restaurants.description: Casual neighborhood cafe serving breakfast, lunch, and baked goods in a welcoming setting.
restaurants.address: East Allendale Road, Saddle River, NJ 07458

restaurants.name: Biagio's Ristorante (nearby)
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian restaurant just across the border in Paramus, popular with Saddle River residents.

parks.name: Saddle River County Park
parks.description: Major Bergen County park following the Saddle River through multiple municipalities. The Saddle River section features walking trails, open fields, and peaceful river views.
parks.address: East Allendale Road, Saddle River, NJ 07458

parks.name: Borough Park
parks.description: Small municipal park adjacent to Borough Hall with a playground and open space for community gatherings.
parks.address: East Allendale Road, Saddle River, NJ 07458

parks.name: Waterfall at Saddle River
parks.description: Scenic natural waterfall along the Saddle River, a hidden gem and popular walking destination.

events.name: Saddle River Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with activities, food, and entertainment for residents and families.

events.name: Holiday Celebration
events.description: Borough-wide holiday event with tree lighting and seasonal festivities.

attractions.name: Saddle River Inn
attractions.description: Award-winning restaurant housed in a historic barn, drawing diners from across the region for its exceptional cuisine and ambiance.

attractions.name: Saddle River Scenic Roads
attractions.description: The winding roads through Saddle River's estate properties offer beautiful drives through wooded landscapes, especially in autumn when the foliage is at its peak.

shoppingAreas: East Allendale Road — Limited local retail
Ramsey Main Street — Nearby shopping and dining
Westwood downtown — Shops and restaurants a short drive away
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major shopping center 15 minutes south

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 2nd Monday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 100 East Allendale Road, Saddle River, NJ 07458

police.address: 100 East Allendale Road, Saddle River, NJ 07458

fire.address: East Allendale Road, Saddle River, NJ 07458

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
